title: The Inner Cut
story: Why aim at edges when you can strike the heart of a word? ciw changes the word under the cursor; diw deletes it — no matter where in the word you stand.

challenges.intro: One word is wrong. Stand anywhere on it and change it to 'right'.
challenges.hint: ciw deletes the whole word under the cursor and enters insert stance.

challenges.intro: A cursed word festers in the line. Cut it out from within.
challenges.hint: diw deletes the inner word — your cursor can be anywhere inside it.
